requiredForPort: Find the equivalent D2R 3.2 routine or data mechanism.
Use strict expected bytes for every memory write.
Prefer a softcoded 3.2 mechanism when one exists.
Validate the resulting behavior in game.

legacyStatusOverrides.name: Sundered MonUMods Patch
legacyStatusOverrides.decision: Do not port the three legacy >=100 to >=99 comparisons.
legacyStatusOverrides.reason: The edit belongs to the customized pre-native Sunder mechanic. D2R 3.2 has native item_pierce_*_immunity stats and states.txt sunderfull/sunder-res-reduce support; the native Sunder result is 95 resistance rather than the legacy custom 99 threshold.

legacyStatusOverrides.name: Make umon field in levels.txt work on all difficulties
legacyStatusOverrides.decision: Removed from the BKVince 3.2 patch set at Vincent's request on 2026-07-18.
legacyStatusOverrides.reason: Keep the vanilla Nightmare/Hell elite-monster selection behavior; do not apply the levels.txt umon pool outside Normal.

legacyStatusOverrides.name: Force max staffmod affixes (part 2)
legacyStatusOverrides.decision: Removed from the BKVince 3.2 patch set at Vincent's request on 2026-07-18.
legacyStatusOverrides.reason: Preserve the vanilla +1 to +3 bonus roll for each staffmod while retaining the separate edit that forces three staffmods.

confirmedExistingPanels.name: Customize Save File Limit
confirmedExistingPanels.description: Allows control of the maximum save file size by replacing the first half of the byte stack only.

supplementalCandidates.name: Expose Item Level to Clients
supplementalCandidates.decision: Cancelled by Vincent on 2026-07-17; do not port.
supplementalCandidates.description: Allows non-hosts in TCP/IP games to see item levels via the loot filter.

supplementalCandidates.name: Display Player Names When Trading
supplementalCandidates.description: Shows player names in the trade window.

supplementalCandidates.name: Allow Character Levels 100+
supplementalCandidates.description: Prevents the Failed to Join error when entering a game with a level 100 or higher character.

supplementalCandidates.name: -Enemy Res% affects immunes
supplementalCandidates.description: Allows the -Enemy Resistance% stat on items and skills to affect immune monsters.
supplementalCandidates.risk: Audit against the native D2R 3.2 Sunder and immunity rules before deciding whether this behavior is still wanted.

supplementalCandidates.name: Autogold: skip first distance check
supplementalCandidates.description: Disables the first distance check in the gold pickup function.

supplementalCandidates.name: Autogold: skip second distance check
supplementalCandidates.description: Disables the second distance check in the same gold pickup function.

supplementalCandidates.name: ITD applies to Champs/Uniques
supplementalCandidates.description: Allows Ignore Target Defense to apply to Champion and Unique monsters.

supplementalCandidates.name: Player Difficulty Scaling Override (Actual)
supplementalCandidates.description: Controls the maximum player difficulty on the server side.
supplementalCandidates.evidenceGap: The legacy UI displays the original value as ???; strict expected bytes must be recovered before any port.

supplementalCandidates.name: Player Difficulty Scaling Override (Command)
supplementalCandidates.description: Controls the maximum player difficulty for the player command.
supplementalCandidates.evidenceGap: The legacy UI displays the original value as ???; strict expected bytes must be recovered before any port.

supplementalCandidates.name: Autogold: always pass cost (data)
supplementalCandidates.description: Sets the stored maximum-range cost value to 2147483647.

supplementalCandidates.name: Player Difficulty Scaling Override (Max/UI)
supplementalCandidates.description: Controls the maximum player difficulty for UI and client checks.

supplementalCandidates.name: Hireling Follow Distance (Moving Owner)
supplementalCandidates.description: Makes Hireable AI follow a walking or running owner from a shorter distance.

supplementalCandidates.name: Hireling Forced Catch-Up Distance
supplementalCandidates.description: Forces Hireable AI to run back to its owner before it wanders too far.

supplementalCandidates.name: Ranged Hireling Activity Cap
supplementalCandidates.description: Raises the non-melee Hireable action chance cap to match melee hirelings.

supplementalCandidates.name: Ranged Hireling Activity Formula Offset
supplementalCandidates.description: Sets the level-formula offset; 49 makes non-melee Hireable AI reach the configured activity cap at every hireling level.

supplementalCandidates.name: Ranged Hireling Retreat Chance
supplementalCandidates.description: Increases the chance for ranged Hireable AI to retreat from a nearby enemy.

supplementalCandidates.name: Ranged Hireling Retreat Distance
supplementalCandidates.description: Starts the ranged Hireable retreat response farther from nearby enemies.

supplementalCandidates.name: Allow CTC while Uninterruptible (On Position)
supplementalCandidates.description: Removes the Uninterruptible-state check from the SkillOnPosition function.

supplementalCandidates.name: Allow CTC while Uninterruptible (On Target)
supplementalCandidates.description: Removes the Uninterruptible-state check from the SkillOnTarget function.

supplementalCandidates.name: Thorns and Burn Kill Credit
supplementalCandidates.description: Credits lethal Thorns and Burning damage to the responsible player, allowing experience and death-trigger events such as CTC missile portals.

targetAnalysis.method: Static comparison of the plaintext 1.2.69270 executable with a read-only process-memory reconstruction of D2R 3.2.92777, followed by manual control-flow and gameplay-semantic review. No process memory was written.

entries.name: Sundered MonUMods Patch
entries.decision: Cancelled. Use the native D2R 3.2 Sunder implementation instead of the legacy custom >=99 threshold.

entries.name: Customize Save File Limit
entries.decision: D2R 3.2 centralizes the old eight writes and already uses the requested 0x8000 limit. No patch is required.

entries.name: Make monsters scale off area level on normal
Make umon field in levels.txt work on all difficulties
entries.decision: Removed from the BKVince 3.2 patch set at Vincent's request; preserve vanilla behavior outside Normal.

sites.futureIntent: Change the LEA delta from -1 to 0.

entries.name: Infinite Town Portal and Identify Tomes

sites.intent: Change the Book-specific delta argument from -1 to 0 before calling the central item and bound-skill quantity routine.

rejectedSites.reason: False positive: the preceding item-type check uses 0x29 (Javelin), not 0x12 (Book).
Rejected as an isolated Book-path write: it bypasses the central bound-skill synchronization used by actual tome consumption.

entries.decision: The exact 3.2 equivalent of the legacy 2.4 patch is the Book-gated call at 0x5817BD: after an explicit item-type 0x12 check, it loads -1 into r9d and calls the central routine at 0x46F090. That routine applies STAT_QUANTITY and synchronizes the bound Book skill. Passing zero preserves both quantities and their packets for TP/ID tomes, while scrolls and weapon quantities remain on their separate paths.

sites.futureIntent: Make the branch unconditional so a 0% value can be displayed.

entries.name: Autogold: send higher cost to pickup function

sites.futureIntent: Change the integer argument from 4 to 20 (raw byte 0x14).

entries.name: Customize Max Character Gold

sites.futureIntent: Change the per-level multiplier from 10000 to 100000 (A0860100).

entries.decision: One 3.2 operand replaces the eighteen historical write sites.

entries.name: Customize Max Stash Gold
entries.futureIntent: Change 2500000 to 25000000 (40787D01) at all three operands.
entries.decision: Three 3.2 operands replace the fifteen historical write sites.

sites.futureIntent: Use Vincent's requested value 6 (06), not vanilla 7.
Change the fixed base count from 3 to 4.
Replace setg bl; inc ebx with mov ebx, 4 (BB04000000).

entries.name: Force max rare affixes
entries.decision: Replace the table result with push 6; pop r12. The four-byte replacement preserves the adjacent xor ebx, ebx initialization.

entries.name: Force max staffmod affixes (part 2)
entries.decision: Do not patch this branch. It selects the +1 to +3 staffmod bonus magnitude; preserving it keeps the vanilla bonus roll while the first edit separately forces three staffmods.

entries.name: Infinite Anya personalize (part 2)
entries.legacyEvidenceCorrection: The legacy binary contains 80604CFD; the old manifest says 8060ACFD.

sites.futureIntent: NOP the Horadric Malus quest-state set call.
NOP the Horadric Malus quest-state clear call.

entries.name: Infinite Larzuk socketing

sites.futureIntent: NOP the first call to the quest 35 completion helper.
NOP the second call to the quest 35 completion helper.

entries.decision: The single legacy caller split into two 3.2 paths.

entries.name: Hit Chance % (Lower Bound)
entries.futureIntent: Change all lower bounds from 5 to 0.

entries.name: Hit Chance % (Upper Bound)
entries.futureIntent: Change all upper bounds from 95 to 100 (64000000).

entries.name: Autogold: allow higher cost in server check

sites.futureIntent: Change the integer limit from 4 to 20 (raw byte 0x14).

entries.name: Expose Item Level to Clients
entries.decision: Cancelled by Vincent; do not research or port.

entries.name: Display Player Names When Trading
entries.decision: D2R 3.2 obtains the player name through the accessor at 0x134FC0. The legacy raw +0xFF structure displacement no longer exists, so there is no honest one-byte equivalent.

entries.name: Allow Character Levels 100+
entries.decision: Bypass the rewritten invalid-level rejection branch; the old one-byte 0x62 threshold is no longer structurally equivalent.

entries.name: -Enemy Res% affects immunes

sites.futureIntent: Make the branch unconditional (EB0A) so the -Enemy Resistance path also runs at resistance >=100.

entries.decision: The immunity check moved into the 0x44F8B0 helper; the branch semantics are otherwise identical to 1.2.69270.

entries.name: Player Difficulty Scaling Override (Actual)

sites.futureIntent: Replace cmovbe eax, ecx with mov eax, ecx; nop (89C890).

entries.decision: This exact 3.2 function writes the shared server difficulty global at RVA 0x2AA6A30.

entries.name: Player Difficulty Scaling Override (Command)
entries.decision: The legacy 17-byte window expanded to 20 bytes because the 3.2 compiler uses r15d/r12d.

entries.name: Autogold: always pass cost (data)
entries.decision: The old RVA 0x1E00890 is an unreferenced runtime/BSS write, not a portable code symbol. In 3.2 the behavior is represented by the explicit server limit and pickup-function argument, so a third blind BSS write would be redundant and unsafe.

entries.name: Player Difficulty Scaling Override (Max/UI)
entries.decision: Patch both lazy initializer paths and both fallback descriptors in code. This preserves the minimum of 1, raises the maximum to 65535, and avoids fragile writes to live BSS objects.

entries.name: Ranged Hireling Activity Formula Offset
entries.decision: The 3.2 compiler folds the legacy offset through a subsequent x2. The legacy requested value 49 therefore becomes the compiled immediate 98 (0x62), preserving the intended formula.

entries.name: Thorns and Burn Kill Credit

sites.role: pack Poison, Open Wounds, and Burning state IDs
scan three packed state IDs and preserve the owner lookup call target

entries.decision: Thorns used result flags 0x4021, whose 0x20 bit suppresses KILLED/KILL events; changing it to 0x4001 restores that path. The lethal periodic-damage owner lookup only scanned Poison (2) and Open Wounds (62); it now also scans Burning (115), whose stat list already retains the applying unit as owner.
